##NAMES=Roscherite
##RRUFFID=R130054
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_2_Mn^2+^_5_Be_4_(PO_4_)_6_(OH)_4_·6H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Tip Top mine, Custer, South Dakota, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Thomas A. Loomis
##DESCRIPTION=Yellowish-brown spheroidal aggregates, associated with R130055 whiteite-(CaMnMg)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R130054
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Ca_1.95_K_0.04_Sr_0.02_Ba_0.01_)_1.02_(Mn_2.77_Fe_0.78_Mg_0.63_Al_0.16_Zn_0.07_)_4.41_Be_4_(P_1.03_O_4_)_6_(OH)_4_·6H_2_O ; Lighter, middle part. Be content assumed ideal.
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 16.006 b: 11.916 c: 6.553 alpha: 90.24 beta: 94.69 gamma: 90.06 volume: 1245.7 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
